title: The Doll
weakest_section: 14:17-15:38 range with awkward date and limited comedy

jokes.text: I was with Marie-Antoinette in a dream last night, and she was on the guillotine, the blade came down, her head fell in the basket, it rolled over, and she started singing Neil Diamond's Solitary Man.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining how we incorporate alarm sounds into dreams
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of a decapitated Marie-Antoinette singing Neil Diamond
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation with the juxtaposition of French Revolution history and Neil Diamond creating an unexpectedly funny image

jokes.text: And I thought, 'That is not my alarm going off. This is actually happening.'
jokes.setup: The ridiculous Marie-Antoinette singing scenario
jokes.punchline: Jerry's deadpan acceptance that this bizarre scene was real
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the absurd dream story with Jerry's characteristic deadpan logic

jokes.text: It's June! It's June! Hey! It's June! It's June, June, June, June! It's June. Ha-ha! It's June! June! Ju-uuu-une! Hey, it's June!
jokes.setup: George's typical neurotic anxiety about his wedding
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top celebratory outburst when wedding is delayed
jokes.explanation: Pure George character comedy - his relief at wedding delay expressed in manic celebration

jokes.text: It's like a stay of execution. Dead man walkin'.
jokes.setup: George celebrating his wedding delay
jokes.punchline: Comparing marriage to death penalty execution
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfect George - his view of marriage as a death sentence with prison execution terminology

jokes.text: Well, one out of 20,000. That's not bad.
jokes.setup: George calling this his lucky day
jokes.punchline: Admitting his terrible luck ratio with characteristic optimism
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-aware pessimism presented as optimism - he knows he's unlucky but finds the bright side

jokes.text: See, that means 'from Vinci.' Did you know that?
jokes.setup: Kramer reading about Leonardo da Vinci
jokes.punchline: Proudly explaining the obvious etymology as if it's profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - presenting common knowledge as if he's discovered something revolutionary

jokes.text: Must be some book.
jokes.setup: Kramer's obvious Leonardo da Vinci revelation
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry sarcastic response to Kramer's pseudo-intellectualism
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry straight-man response - economical sarcasm deflating Kramer's pretension

jokes.text: Which means if I live to be 80, I will have lived the equivalent of 105 years.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining da Vinci's sleep schedule giving extra waking time
jokes.punchline: The convoluted math and logic of 'living' extra years by sleeping less
jokes.explanation: Kramer's pseudo-scientific reasoning taken to absurd mathematical conclusion

jokes.text: I got a lot of things in the hopper.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about what Kramer will accomplish with extra time
jokes.punchline: Kramer's vague claim about having projects
jokes.explanation: Setup for the next joke - Kramer's typical grandiose but undefined plans

jokes.text: I didn't know you had one. / Oh, I got a hopper. A big hopper.
jokes.setup: Jerry's deadpan response to Kramer's 'hopper' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Kramer doubling down on the hopper metaphor literally
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry-Kramer dynamic - Jerry's literal interpretation of metaphor, Kramer's earnest doubling down

jokes.text: Poor bastard.
jokes.setup: New employee Bob being assigned fact-checking work as 'baptism by fire'
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sympathetic aside about the doomed new guy
jokes.explanation: Elaine's workplace cynicism - knowing what's coming for the new employee

jokes.text: Could you repeat that? / Why don't you handle all the copyediting? / I'm... I'm sorry. What? / Copyediting. / Uh, never mind. / Elaine, you do it.
jokes.setup: Bob being assigned work but claiming hearing problems
jokes.punchline: The awkward escalation as Peterman gives up and dumps work on Elaine
jokes.explanation: Workplace cringe comedy - the hearing impairment scam setting up future jokes

jokes.text: How embarrassing this must be for you. You just bought your own dinner.
jokes.setup: Jerry doesn't have proper jacket for Friars Club dining room
jokes.punchline: Host's condescending comment about Jerry paying for dinner as punishment
jokes.explanation: Elite club snobbery - the weird punishment system of exclusive establishments

jokes.text: Ha-ho! Funny. Isn't he funny? Funny guy.
jokes.setup: The host's condescending treatment
jokes.punchline: Jerry's uncomfortable forced laughter and overcompensation
jokes.explanation: Jerry's social discomfort when trying to fit into exclusive club atmosphere

jokes.text: No, the stupidest thing you ever said was when Steve Kroft from 60 Minutes is the same guy from Seals & Croft.
jokes.setup: George claiming he could coach in the NFL
jokes.punchline: Jerry bringing up George's previous absurd celebrity confusion
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing George's history of confident incorrect statements

jokes.text: I'm percolating, Jerry. I'm telling you, I have never felt so fertile. I'm mossy, Jerry. My brain is mossy.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking if the da Vinci sleep schedule is working
jokes.punchline: Kramer's bizarre metaphors for mental creativity
jokes.explanation: Peak Kramer dialogue - 'mossy brain' is wonderfully absurd and perfectly in character

jokes.text: A restaurant that serves only peanut butter and jelly. What do you call it? PB and J's. What do you think?
jokes.setup: Kramer claiming enhanced creativity from sleep schedule
jokes.punchline: His terrible restaurant concept with obvious name
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer business idea - confidently presented terrible concept that sounds like it came from a sleep-deprived mind

jokes.text: I think you need more sleep.
jokes.setup: Kramer's PB&J restaurant idea
jokes.punchline: Jerry's deadpan assessment contradicting Kramer's sleep theory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry straight-man line - economical deflation of Kramer's theory

jokes.text: Why? What time is it? / It's 4:00. / Four in the morning? / Yeah. / What's wrong with you? / I'm bored.
jokes.setup: Kramer waking Jerry up
jokes.punchline: The absurd revelation that Kramer is just bored at 4 AM
jokes.explanation: Kramer's sleep schedule backfiring - the unintended consequence of having too much free time

jokes.text: Every time Mr. Peterman tries to assign him any work, he says he can't hear. And it all gets dumped on me.
jokes.setup: Elaine complaining about Bob Grossberg
jokes.punchline: Revealing Bob's hearing impairment scam affecting Elaine's workload
jokes.explanation: Workplace comedy - the hearing impairment scam's effect on coworkers

jokes.text: I... I'm sorry. What? / I'm kind of swamped. / Thank you.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking Bob to help with catalog work
jokes.punchline: Bob's fake hearing problem causing him to miss the request entirely
jokes.explanation: Running gag development - Bob's hearing scam in action with increasingly absurd responses

jokes.text: Somehow I thought he'd be taller.
jokes.setup: Elaine pointing out Bob to Jerry
jokes.punchline: Jerry's anticlimactic observation about Bob's height
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry deflation - focusing on irrelevant physical detail instead of the scam

jokes.text: Hey, Bob. Bob? Hey, Bobby, over here. Bob? Oh, Bob? Bob?
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to get Bob's attention
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly desperate attempts to get a response from 'deaf' Bob
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through dialogue - Elaine's frustration building as she tries different approaches

jokes.text: I snuck up behind him at the urinal and tried to see if he could hear me.
jokes.setup: Jerry testing Bob's hearing
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate bathroom setting for the hearing test
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Jerry's commitment to detective work in the most awkward setting possible

jokes.text: Well...he flinched...sort of. / What do you mean, sort of? What did he do? / Well, he kind of moved his head, you know? But it might have been on the zip-up, I don't know.
jokes.setup: Elaine eager for results of Jerry's hearing test
jokes.punchline: Jerry's inconclusive results due to bathroom timing complications
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of bathroom cringe - the ambiguous evidence making the whole scheme more absurd

jokes.text: We could be like the Gatsbys. Didn't they always have, like, a bunch of people around, and they were all best friends? / That doesn't sound right.
jokes.setup: George excited about double dating with Jerry and Hallie
jokes.punchline: George's complete misunderstanding of The Great Gatsby's social dynamics
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - confidently referencing literature he clearly doesn't understand, with Jerry's gentle correction

jokes.text: Somehow I dozed off and woke up in a pile of garbage. / Somehow?
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining why the jacket got dirty
jokes.punchline: Jerry's incredulous reaction to Kramer's casual explanation of ending up in garbage
jokes.explanation: Jerry's perfect incredulous response to Kramer's bizarre sleep-deprivation consequences

jokes.text: I want you so bad, Bob. You turn me on so much. You're so damn sexy. Ooh. I'm starting to unbutton. Anything getting through?
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to test Bob's hearing with increasingly obvious methods
jokes.punchline: Her sexual come-on as the ultimate hearing test
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Elaine's desperation leading to inappropriate workplace seduction as hearing test

jokes.text: I feel like Lincoln. / Well, let's hope this evening turns out a little better.
jokes.setup: Jerry commenting on their theater box seats
jokes.punchline: Hallie's dark reference to Lincoln's assassination at the theater
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor callback to Lincoln's theater assassination - perfectly timed and economical

jokes.text: Well, this is, uh, risky business, huh? I'm all atwitter.
jokes.setup: Kramer and Connie on their strange indoor date
jokes.punchline: Kramer's awkward attempts at romantic banter with odd word choices
jokes.explanation: Kramer's social awkwardness - his forced romantic dialogue feels unnatural and comedically stilted

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Cosmo, wake up. Cosmo? Oh, my God. He's dead! He's dead.
jokes.setup: Kramer collapsing during intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Connie's panicked assumption that Kramer has died during sex
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation - sleep deprivation leading to apparent death during intimacy

jokes.text: Yeah, Tommy, this is Connie. You gotta help me. Some guy dropped dead on top of me. I can't call the cops 'cause Joey might find out.
jokes.setup: Connie thinking Kramer is dead
jokes.punchline: Her criminal panic call revealing she's cheating and involved with shady people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation revealing Connie's criminal connections and the real reason she won't be seen with Kramer

jokes.text: Nyea.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about his jacket
jokes.punchline: Magician's dismissive grunt as response
jokes.explanation: Simple visual/audio comedy - the magician's unintelligible response to Jerry's reasonable request

jokes.text: Broke a shoelace today. / Oh, I can get you shoelaces tomorrow. / Okay. / So, what color? / Brown. Maybe a black. / More coffee? / No, check...please.
jokes.setup: George and Hallie on their awkward date
jokes.punchline: The painfully mundane conversation about shoelaces leading to abrupt date ending
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - the most boring possible conversation topic leading to date death

jokes.text: Hey! Sha! Mama! No! Help!
jokes.setup: Kramer waking up in the Hudson River
jokes.punchline: His panicked exclamations as he realizes his situation
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy through dialogue - Kramer's awakening to attempted murder situation

jokes.text: I heard every word you said. And I know you wouldn't be just having fun with his handicap. Ooh, that kind of cruelty would be grounds for dismissal.
jokes.setup: Peterman overhearing Elaine's seductive hearing test
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding thinking she was being considerate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ironic misunderstanding - Peterman interpreting sexual harassment as disability sensitivity

jokes.text: Frankly, I didn't think she was too concerned about my jacket.
jokes.setup: George asking why Jerry is soured on Hallie
jokes.punchline: Jerry's petty focus on jacket indifference as relationship dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry pettiness - ending relationship over perceived lack of concern for his minor problem

jokes.text: You don't try and de-sour. You have to sweeten too. / I'll try. / I'll try and de-sour and sweeten.
jokes.setup: Jerry agreeing to de-sour if jacket is returned
jokes.punchline: George's escalating demands for attitude adjustment using food metaphors
jokes.explanation: George's relationship management through food-based emotional terminology

jokes.text: I wanna get it back to when we were the Gatsbys. / I still don't know what that means.
jokes.setup: George still pushing the Gatsby comparison
jokes.punchline: Jerry's continued confusion about George's literary reference
jokes.explanation: Callback to George's literary confusion with Jerry's honest bewilderment

jokes.text: But I woke up in the Hudson River in a sack!
jokes.setup: Kramer claiming Connie tried to kill him
jokes.punchline: The dramatic revelation of attempted mob-style execution
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - from casual dating to mob execution attempt, delivered with Kramer's matter-of-fact style

jokes.text: She doesn't want anybody else to have me!
jokes.setup: Jerry asking why Connie would try to kill Kramer
jokes.punchline: Kramer's narcissistic interpretation of attempted murder as romantic jealousy
jokes.explanation: Peak Kramer ego - interpreting attempted murder as extreme romantic devotion

jokes.text: Oh, you go into a department store, you put a suit on, and you walk right out. What are you, some sort of an idiot?
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining he forgot to return the jacket
jokes.punchline: Pat Cooper's escalating incredulity and insults about Jerry's jacket theft
jokes.explanation: Pat Cooper's aggressive comedy style - treating accidental jacket retention as criminal shoplifting

jokes.text: Well, one of the Gypsies took it. / Oh, the Gypsies took it. Of course, New York has a lot of Gypsies. Ooh, on every block, there's a Gypsy.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining the magicians took his jacket
jokes.punchline: Pat Cooper's sarcastic response about Gypsy stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Pat Cooper's sarcastic dismissal of Jerry's excuse with ethnic stereotype commentary

jokes.text: Are you an entertainer? Are you in show business? / No, I... / Then what am I talking to you for?
jokes.setup: Someone trying to talk to Pat Cooper
jokes.punchline: Pat's brutal dismissal based on professional status
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Pat Cooper's industry elitism and rudeness in natural habitat

jokes.text: I think this crest is different. It's got a moose on it. / Moose? / Yeah. / I don't think this is the jacket.
jokes.setup: Jerry and George confronting man wearing similar jacket
jokes.punchline: Discovering it's a different jacket with moose instead of club crest
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - the jacket confrontation revealing mistaken identity through animal crest detail

jokes.text: Great. Now she's sour. / Maybe she'll sweeten. / She won't sweeten. And I'm bitter!
jokes.setup: Hallie leaving after returning the jacket
jokes.punchline: Jerry's emotional state escalating through food metaphors from sour to bitter
jokes.explanation: Callback to food emotion metaphors with Jerry's escalation to bitter completing the taste progression

jokes.text: What, sleeping with the fishes? I guess I woke up.
jokes.setup: Connie shocked to see Kramer alive
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual pun on mob execution terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kramer line - casual wordplay with mob terminology while revealing survival from attempted murder

jokes.text: Attempted murder? Of who? / This guy, Kramer. / Whoa. Cosmo Kramer? / Yeah, that's right. / I don't want nothing to do with it.
jokes.setup: Connie calling her lawyer for attempted murder charge
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's immediate refusal upon hearing Kramer's name
jokes.explanation: Great final escalation - even criminal lawyers won't touch a case involving Kramer

tmdb_overview: Susan's friend interferes with Jerry's planned jokes; Susan's doll reminds George of his mother; Frank Costanza builds a billiard room.
